greek: ὅτι καὶ αὐτὴ ἡ κτίσις ἐλευθερωθήσεται ἀπὸ τῆς δουλείας τῆς φθορᾶς εἰς τὴν ἐλευθερίαν τῆς δόξης τῶν τέκνων τοῦ θεοῦ.
kjv: Because the creature itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the glorious liberty of the children of God.
modern: that the creation itself also will be set free from its bondage to decay into the freedom of the glory of the children of God.

greek: τὸ γὰρ φρόνημα τῆς σαρκὸς θάνατος, τὸ δὲ φρόνημα τοῦ πνεύματος ζωὴ καὶ εἰρήνη·
kjv: For to be carnally minded is death; but to be spiritually minded is life and peace.
modern: For the mindset of the flesh is death, but the mindset of the Spirit is life and peace.

greek: διότι τὸ φρόνημα τῆς σαρκὸς ἔχθρα εἰς θεόν, τῷ γὰρ νόμῳ τοῦ θεοῦ οὐχ ὑποτάσσεται, οὐδὲ γὰρ δύναται·
kjv: Because the carnal mind is enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
modern: because the mindset of the flesh is hostility toward God; for it does not submit to the law of God—indeed, it is not able to.

greek: τῇ γὰρ ματαιότητι ἡ κτίσις ὑπετάγη, οὐχ ἑκοῦσα ἀλλὰ διὰ τὸν ὑποτάξαντα, ἐφ’ ἑλπίδι
kjv: For the creature was made subject to vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected the same in hope,
modern: For to futility the creation was subjected, not willingly but because of the one who subjected it, in hope.

greek: ἡ γὰρ ἀποκαραδοκία τῆς κτίσεως τὴν ἀποκάλυψιν τῶν υἱῶν τοῦ θεοῦ ἀπεκδέχεται·
kjv: For the earnest expectation of the creature waiteth for the manifestation of the sons of God.
modern: For the eager longing of the creation waits for the revelation of the sons of God.

greek: οἱ γὰρ κατὰ σάρκα ὄντες τὰ τῆς σαρκὸς φρονοῦσιν, οἱ δὲ κατὰ πνεῦμα τὰ τοῦ πνεύματος.
kjv: For they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
modern: For those who are according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who are according to the Spirit on the things of the Spirit.

greek: τίς ἐγκαλέσει κατὰ ἐκλεκτῶν θεοῦ; θεὸς ὁ δικαιῶν·
kjv: Who shall lay any thing to the charge of God’s elect? It is God that justifieth.
modern: Who will bring a charge against God’s elect? God is the one who justifies.

greek: Ὑμεῖς δὲ οὐκ ἐστὲ ἐν σαρκὶ ἀλλὰ ἐν πνεύματι, εἴπερ πνεῦμα θεοῦ οἰκεῖ ἐν ὑμῖν. εἰ δέ τις πνεῦμα Χριστοῦ οὐκ ἔχει, οὗτος οὐκ ἔστιν αὐτοῦ.
kjv: But ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if so be that the Spirit of God dwell in you. Now if any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of his.
modern: You, however, are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if indeed the Spirit of God dwells in you. But if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, this person does not belong to him.

greek: πέπεισμαι γὰρ ὅτι οὔτε θάνατος οὔτε ζωὴ οὔτε ἄγγελοι οὔτε ἀρχαὶ οὔτε ἐνεστῶτα οὔτε μέλλοντα οὔτε δυνάμεις
kjv: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
modern: For I am convinced that neither death nor life nor angels nor rulers nor things present nor things to come nor powers

greek: ὅς γε τοῦ ἰδίου υἱοῦ οὐκ ἐφείσατο, ἀλλὰ ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν πάντων παρέδωκεν αὐτόν, πῶς οὐχὶ καὶ σὺν αὐτῷ τὰ πάντα ἡμῖν χαρίσεται;
kjv: He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?
modern: He indeed did not spare his own Son but handed him over for us all; how will he not also, with him, graciously give us all things?

greek: ἀλλ’ ἐν τούτοις πᾶσιν ὑπερνικῶμεν διὰ τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντος ἡμᾶς.
kjv: Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us.
modern: But in all these things we are more than conquerors through the one who loved us.

greek: εἰ γὰρ κατὰ σάρκα ζῆτε μέλλετε ἀποθνῄσκειν, εἰ δὲ πνεύματι τὰς πράξεις τοῦ σώματος θανατοῦτε, ζήσεσθε.
kjv: For if ye live after the flesh, ye shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
modern: For if you live according to the flesh, you are going to die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.

greek: εἰ δὲ ὃ οὐ βλέπομεν ἐλπίζομεν, δι’ ὑπομονῆς ἀπεκδεχόμεθα.
kjv: But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.
modern: But if we hope for what we do not see, we wait for it with endurance.

greek: Οὐδὲν ἄρα νῦν κατάκριμα τοῖς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ·
kjv: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
modern: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.

greek: οἱ δὲ ἐν σαρκὶ ὄντες θεῷ ἀρέσαι οὐ δύνανται.
kjv: So then they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
modern: Those who are in the flesh cannot please God.

greek: ὅσοι γὰρ πνεύματι θεοῦ ἄγονται, οὗτοι υἱοί εἰσιν θεοῦ.
kjv: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.
modern: For all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.

greek: τίς ὁ κατακρινῶν; Χριστὸς ὁ ἀποθανών, μᾶλλον δὲ ἐγερθείς, ὅς καί ἐστιν ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ θεοῦ, ὃς καὶ ἐντυγχάνει ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν·
kjv: Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
modern: Who is to condemn? Christ is the one who died, or rather, who was raised, who is also at the right hand of God, who also intercedes for us.

greek: οὐ γὰρ ἐλάβετε πνεῦμα δουλείας πάλιν εἰς φόβον, ἀλλὰ ἐλάβετε πνεῦμα υἱοθεσίας ἐν ᾧ κράζομεν· Αββα ὁ πατήρ·
kjv: For ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
modern: For you did not receive a spirit of slavery again leading to fear, but you received a spirit of adoption, by which we cry, Abba, Father.

greek: ὁ γὰρ νόμος τοῦ πνεύματος τῆς ζωῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ ἠλευθέρωσέν σε ἀπὸ τοῦ νόμου τῆς ἁμαρτίας καὶ τοῦ θανάτου.
kjv: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.
modern: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.

greek: ἵνα τὸ δικαίωμα τοῦ νόμου πληρωθῇ ἐν ἡμῖν τοῖς μὴ κατὰ σάρκα περιπατοῦσιν ἀλλὰ κατὰ πνεῦμα·
kjv: That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
modern: so that the righteous requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.

greek: τῇ γὰρ ἐλπίδι ἐσώθημεν· ἐλπὶς δὲ βλεπομένη οὐκ ἔστιν ἐλπίς, ὃ γὰρ βλέπει τίς ἐλπίζει;
kjv: For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
modern: For in hope we were saved; but hope that is seen is not hope, for who hopes for what he sees?

greek: ὁ δὲ ἐραυνῶν τὰς καρδίας οἶδεν τί τὸ φρόνημα τοῦ πνεύματος, ὅτι κατὰ θεὸν ἐντυγχάνει ὑπὲρ ἁγίων.
kjv: And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.
modern: And the one who searches hearts knows what the mind of the Spirit is, because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to God.

greek: ὅτι οὓς προέγνω, καὶ προώρισεν συμμόρφους τῆς εἰκόνος τοῦ υἱοῦ αὐτοῦ, εἰς τὸ εἶναι αὐτὸν πρωτότοκον ἐν πολλοῖς ἀδελφοῖς·
kjv: For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
modern: For those whom he foreknew, he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, so that he might be the firstborn among many brothers and sisters.

greek: οὓς δὲ προώρισεν, τούτους καὶ ἐκάλεσεν· καὶ οὓς ἐκάλεσεν, τούτους καὶ ἐδικαίωσεν· οὓς δὲ ἐδικαίωσεν, τούτους καὶ ἐδόξασεν.
kjv: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
modern: And those whom he predestined, these he also called; and those whom he called, these he also justified; and those whom he justified, these he also glorified.

greek: Ὡσαύτως δὲ καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα συναντιλαμβάνεται τῇ ἀσθενείᾳ ἡμῶν· τὸ γὰρ τί προσευξώμεθα καθὸ δεῖ οὐκ οἴδαμεν, ἀλλὰ αὐτὸ τὸ πνεῦμα ὑπερεντυγχάνει στεναγμοῖς ἀλαλήτοις,
kjv: Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.
modern: Likewise, the Spirit also helps us in our weakness; for we do not know what we should pray for as we ought, but the Spirit himself intercedes with inexpressible groanings.

greek: αὐτὸ τὸ πνεῦμα συμμαρτυρεῖ τῷ πνεύματι ἡμῶν ὅτι ἐσμὲν τέκνα θεοῦ.
kjv: The Spirit itself beareth witness with our spirit, that we are the children of God:
modern: The Spirit himself bears witness with our spirit that we are children of God.

greek: Ἄρα οὖν, ἀδελφοί, ὀφειλέται ἐσμέν, οὐ τῇ σαρκὶ τοῦ κατὰ σάρκα ζῆν,
kjv: Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
modern: So then, brothers and sisters, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.

greek: οἴδαμεν γὰρ ὅτι πᾶσα ἡ κτίσις συστενάζει καὶ συνωδίνει ἄχρι τοῦ νῦν·
kjv: For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
modern: For we know that the whole creation has been groaning together and suffering labor pains together until now.

greek: εἰ δὲ τέκνα, καὶ κληρονόμοι· κληρονόμοι μὲν θεοῦ, συγκληρονόμοι δὲ Χριστοῦ, εἴπερ συμπάσχομεν ἵνα καὶ συνδοξασθῶμεν.
kjv: And if children, then heirs; heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.
modern: and if children, then heirs—heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ, provided that we suffer with him so that we may also be glorified with him.

greek: Λογίζομαι γὰρ ὅτι οὐκ ἄξια τὰ παθήματα τοῦ νῦν καιροῦ πρὸς τὴν μέλλουσαν δόξαν ἀποκαλυφθῆναι εἰς ἡμᾶς.
kjv: For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
modern: For I consider that the sufferings of the present time are not worth comparing with the glory about to be revealed to us.

greek: εἰ δὲ τὸ πνεῦμα τοῦ ἐγείραντος τὸν Ἰησοῦν ἐκ νεκρῶν οἰκεῖ ἐν ὑμῖν, ὁ ἐγείρας ἐκ νεκρῶν Χριστὸν Ἰησοῦν ζῳοποιήσει καὶ τὰ θνητὰ σώματα ὑμῶν διὰ τὸ ἐνοικοῦν αὐτοῦ πνεῦμα ἐν ὑμῖν.
kjv: But if the Spirit of him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, he that raised up Christ from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his Spirit that dwelleth in you.
modern: But if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, he who raised Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies because of his Spirit dwelling in you.

greek: τίς ἡμᾶς χωρίσει ἀπὸ τῆς ἀγάπης τοῦ Χριστοῦ; θλῖψις ἢ στενοχωρία ἢ διωγμὸς ἢ λιμὸς ἢ γυμνότης ἢ κίνδυνος ἢ μάχαιρα;
kjv: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
modern: Who will separate us from the love of Christ? Will affliction, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?

greek: οὔτε ὕψωμα οὔτε βάθος οὔτε τις κτίσις ἑτέρα δυνήσεται ἡμᾶς χωρίσαι ἀπὸ τῆς ἀγάπης τοῦ θεοῦ τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ τῷ κυρίῳ ἡμῶν.
kjv: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
modern: nor height nor depth, nor any other created thing, will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.

greek: καθὼς γέγραπται ὅτι Ἕνεκεν σοῦ θανατούμεθα ὅλην τὴν ἡμέραν, ἐλογίσθημεν ὡς πρόβατα σφαγῆς.
kjv: As it is written, For thy sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
modern: As it is written: For your sake we are being put to death all day long; we were counted as sheep for slaughter.

greek: Οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι τοῖς ἀγαπῶσι τὸν θεὸν πάντα συνεργεῖ εἰς ἀγαθόν, τοῖς κατὰ πρόθεσιν κλητοῖς οὖσιν.
kjv: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
modern: And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good, for those who are called according to his purpose.

greek: εἰ δὲ Χριστὸς ἐν ὑμῖν, τὸ μὲν σῶμα νεκρὸν διὰ ἁμαρτίαν, τὸ δὲ πνεῦμα ζωὴ διὰ δικαιοσύνην.
kjv: And if Christ be in you, the body is dead because of sin; but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.
modern: But if Christ is in you, the body is indeed dead because of sin, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.

greek: οὐ μόνον δέ, ἀλλὰ καὶ αὐτοὶ τὴν ἀπαρχὴν τοῦ πνεύματος ἔχοντες ἡμεῖς καὶ αὐτοὶ ἐν ἑαυτοῖς στενάζομεν, υἱοθεσίαν ἀπεκδεχόμενοι τὴν ἀπολύτρωσιν τοῦ σώματος ἡμῶν.
kjv: And not only they, but ourselves also, which have the firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the adoption, to wit, the redemption of our body.
modern: And not only that, but we ourselves, who have the firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, as we eagerly await adoption, the redemption of our body.

greek: Τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν πρὸς ταῦτα; εἰ ὁ θεὸς ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν, τίς καθ’ ἡμῶν;
kjv: What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who can be against us?
modern: What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who is against us?

greek: τὸ γὰρ ἀδύνατον τοῦ νόμου, ἐν ᾧ ἠσθένει διὰ τῆς σαρκός, ὁ θεὸς τὸν ἑαυτοῦ υἱὸν πέμψας ἐν ὁμοιώματι σαρκὸς ἁμαρτίας καὶ περὶ ἁμαρτίας κατέκρινε τὴν ἁμαρτίαν ἐν τῇ σαρκί,
kjv: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
modern: For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God did by sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin: he condemned sin in the flesh,
